observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Instruct the accountant to prepare and submit Bank Reconciliation Statements of all the bank accounts maintained for the period January to December 2024 and thereafter submit BRS within 15 days after the end of each month as required by COA Circular No. 96-011; and
Revisit current staffing situation at the municipal accounting office to verify issue on manpower shortage to ensure that Bank Reconciliation Statements are prepared and submitted on time.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Secure Approved Travel Order and Itinerary of Travel before granting of Cash Advance to Officers and Employees;
Enjoin the Municipal Accountant to stop the granting of additional 	cash advance unless the previous cash advance given is first settled or 	proper accounting thereof is made; and
Demand immediate settlement of cash advances and/or cause the 	withholding of the amount due from the concerned officials and 	employees to compel them to settle their accounts. Management may 	resort to legal action if no settlement is made after sending three (3) 	demand letters.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Enjoin the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with all department heads and retrieve all supporting documents for unpaid liabilities of the 	Municipality that may be in their custody and validate the documents 	that may be gathered;
Send notices to the creditors affected for possible claim, and
If still not claimed within reasonable period of time, cause reversion to 	the Unappropriated Surplus pursuant to Section 98 of Presidential 	Decree No. 1445 for fair presentation of the account balance in the 	financial statements.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Direct the Accountant to secure Certificates of Full Payment for all settled loans and submit copies to the audit team;
Direct the Accounting unit to undertake a thorough review and reconciliation of the Loans Payable – Domestic account to trace the origin of the discrepancy and ensure that all transactions are properly 	accounted for; and
Upon securing the necessary documents and reconciliation, make appropriate correcting entries to correct the Loans Payable – Domestic account. Any corrections should be supported with proper documentation and be disclosed in the notes to financial statements.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to instruct the Municipal Accountant for the immediate submission of disbursement vouchers (DVs) and Liquidation Reports with its supporting documents and to ensure that future submissions are made within the prescribed period set by COA Circular No. 2009-006.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Establish clear policies and guidelines for the hiring, selection, compensation, and management of Job Order (JO) personnel.
Execute formal contracts for all Job Order (JO) personnel that clearly 	outlines the specific duties and responsibilities, office assignments, 	wages rate, work schedule and duration of employment.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:
Given the enormity of workload involving one time-cleansing of PPE, consider implementing the PPE cleansing by phase (i.e. by PPE class);
Enjoin the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain Property Ledger Cards and to derecognize properties that do not qualify the capitalization threshold of PPE; and
Instruct the GSO to prepare Property Stock Cards and ensure that future issuances of properties that are within and above the capitalization threshold are issued with Property Acknowledgement Receipt (PAR).

We recommended that management:
Remit to the Bureau of Treasury the long outstanding balance and submit proof thereof to COA along with copies of the JEV taking up the remittance. Likewise, submit proof of declination by the Bureau of treasury, if remittance is refused; and
After exhausting all means of returning the funds to the Bureau of Treasury, request for write-off dormant Due to NGAs items following the guidelines prescribed under COA Circular 2016-005.
We recommended that management stop the practice of charging prior year’s expenses against the current year appropriations, and spend within the budget.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant comply with the requirement of COA Circular No. 2012-002 by establishing procedures which will ensure that cash pertaining to the unexpended balance of LDRRMF is actually transferred to the Trust Fund at year-end.

observations.managementAction: Management to start one-time cleansing of PPE per account and will perform the recommendation starting this year.
observations.reason: Time constraint amongst the members of the inventory committee

observations.managementAction: To perform the recommendation within CY 2024.
observations.reason: Numerous task to be performed by the accounting unit.

observations.managementAction: Management commit to perform the recommendations within CY 2024.
observations.reason: Time constraints on the management to perform the recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We revised our recommendation that management provide justification that the honoraria or allowance received by the MBOC is not similar to the honorarium or allowance being given by the COMELEC, otherwise it shall be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: Management transmitted a letter to the COMELEC for the legal basis on the grant
observations.reason: The audit team will issue a Notice of Disallowance on this regard if justification will not be submitted timely.

observations.reason: The GAD Focal Person has not submitted her GAD Accomplishment Report, for verification by the audit team

The GSO Designate has three working staff that could have attended to the deficiencies noted

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management instruct the BAC Secretariat to post the required information under Item 7.1 of GPPB Circular 02-2021, for Ancillary Supplies and Services contracts entered into, reckoned from the circular’s effectivity.
We recommended that management adheres to the principle of segregation of duties by separating the request, canvass, and approval function in its purchases.
We recommended that management provide the necessary specifications on the items to be procured for similar procurements yet to be undertaken.

We recommended that management enjoin the MHO, the MBO, and the members of the Sangguniang Bayan to review the adequacy and reasonableness of Hazard Pay appropriation in the C.Y. 2022 Annual Budget, in the hope that the PHWs will enjoy the full entitlement of the benefit due them.
We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjustments on the affected accounts.
We recommended that the municipal accountant draw an adjusting entry for the reclassification.

observations.reason: The GSO, the RHU Personnel and the Municipal Accountant committed to observe the proper recording and inventory management. | Post-audit of transactions covering C.Y. 2021 revealed the outright recording of expenses for drugs and medicines

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management instruct the Supply Officer thru coordination with the Municipal Health Officer and the LDRRM Officer to submit to the OCD and the COA a complete list of donations received and distributed. Likewise, we recommended that management streamline and document its donation process to better establish accountability and centralize accounting of donated goods.
observations.managementAction: Management concurred with the recommendation on the streamlining of the donation process by way of issuing an Executive Order. Moreover, the Supply Officer committed to submit the required report and supporting documents for the donations in-kind.
observations.reason: The GSO has submitted the List of Donations In-Kind as well as the Distribution List; However, management failed to streamline and documents its donation process; hence, the audit finding is reiterated with modification

observations.recommendation:
Instruct the GSO to prepare a complete inventory report accounting the undistributed welfare goods and explain the reasons for the variances cited;
Organize a team which may be composed of the GSO, Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, and barangay officials concerned to verify absence of signatures of the recipients, and invite this Commission to serve as witness during the validation.
observations.managementAction: Management shared that they were able to obtain affidavits of the receipt by the beneficiaries cited in the AOM. However, in compliance with the recommendation, they will form a validation team and will invite the audit team for the on-site verification.
Management concurred with the audit recommendations and will confer with officials of DSWD- Region 10 on the matter.
observations.reason:
Management sought a clarification from the DSWD- Regional Office No. 10. 

DSWD informed the MSWDO that it was already incumbent upon the LGU to verify the eligibility of the listed beneficiaries. 

Management reasoned that the persons identified in the audit report were equally affected by the pandemic, financially, more so mentally as they live alone.

observations.managementAction: Management concurred with the audit recommendation and will confer with officials of DSWD- Region 10 on the matter.
observations.reason:
Management sought a clarification from the DSWD- Regional Office No. 10. 

DSWD informed the MSWDO that it was already incumbent upon the LGU to verify the eligibility of the listed beneficiaries. 

As such, a Notice of Disallowance will be issued by the audit team to payees who were clearly duplicates.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management instruct the Supply Officer thru coordination with the Municipal Health Officer and the LDRRM Officer to submit to the OCD and the COA a complete list of donations received and distributed. Likewise, we recommended that management streamline and document its donation process to better establish accountability and centralize accounting of donated goods.

We recommended that management:
Revisit the staffing pattern of the municipal accounting office for recruitment of staff or temporary augmentation of personnel;
Instruct the municipal accountant for the immediate submission of the updated financial reports and documents; and
Enjoin the Municipal Accountant to strictly enforce the submission of succeeding required financial reports and accounts within the prescribed period.

We recommended that management instruct the MPDC and the Municipal Engineer to coordinate closely in planning and implementing development programs and projects. Likewise, we recommended reprogramming of budget for projects that are no longer intended or practicable to be implemented for optimal utilization of the fund.

We recommended that management submit a justification with supporting documents detailing theoffice order and actual attendancein hospitals and healthcare facilities, the work rendered and computation of the SRAreceived by payees; otherwise,we recommend that management direct the persons liable for the refund of the amount paid.

We recommended that management:

Direct the Municipal Treasurer with the Local Chief Executive to make a representation to the Department of Finance requesting reconsideration and restructure on the penalties charged for possible reduction of the amount;

Enjoin the responsible officials in the monitoring of loans payable of the LGU to submit a written explanation thru the Local Chief Executive, why the penalty of P1,534,943.58 for late payments of DOF loan should not be a personal liability on their part; and

Direct the Municipal Accountant to adjust the financial statements to recognize the unpaid penalties after setting-up appropriate cash back-up for the obligation.
observations.managementAction: Management wrote a letter to the DOF asking for reconsideration on the penalties
observations.reason: - The municipality sent a letter to DOF on the reconsideration of the penalties, however their request was denied. The concerned officials are yet to submit their justification on why they should not be held personally liable thereon.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

Appropriate budget to satisfy obligations as agreed in the Compromise Agreement; and 

 Direct the Municipal Accountant to adjust the financial statements to reflect the rights and obligations of the LGU on the Compromise Agreement entered into by the LGU and the plaintiff to settle the Civil Case No. 5316 on payment of just compensation of the land and damages.
observations.managementAction: Management provided for appropriation for the payment of damages
observations.reason: - the municipal accountant provided adequate disclosure. Meanwhile, an appropriation was allotted in CY 2020 for the payment of the damages.

observations.recommendation:

Require the Municipal Accountant to effect adjusting entries to rectify the noted overstatement/understatement of the SET Receivables and their related accounts and observe the correct accounting entries in recording SET collections; and 

Enjoin the Municipal Treasurer to regularly report to the Municipal Accountant the total amount of discounts and penalties on the RPT and SET as basis for recording to their appropriate accounts.

Stop the practice of giving fixed Subsistence Allowances; and

Enjoin the Municipal Accountant to strictly follow the rules and regulations on the grant of Subsistence and Laundry Allowances to Public Health Workers and Subsistence Allowance for Public Social Workers in accordance with DBM-DOH Joint Circular No. 1 series of 2012 and DBM-DSWD Joint Circular No. 1 series of 2018, respectively, considering the actual number of workdays rendered as well as the enumerated circumstances for non-entitlement.

observations.managementAction: The LGU acquired necessary IT equipment to be used for the establishment of GAD Database.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management committed to secure from the Philippine Councilors’ League Incorporated, Vice Mayors’ League of the Philippines, and League of Municipalities of the Philippines to issue a government official receipt for all payments made to establish proper financial accountability.
observations.reason: The LGU is waiting for the National Officers of the Leagues of the local elective officials resolve the issue since the LGU is dependent on their action.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed to strictly comply with COA Circular No. 2009-001 to submit all copies of perfected contracts and purchase orders along with the required relevant supporting documents within five (5) working days from execution/approval; and

To strictly enforce the provisions of Section 16, Rule V of RA 9184 and its revised Implementing Rules and Regulations on the professionalization of BAC, TWG Members, and Procurement Units, whereby the HoPE shall ensure that they are sent to attend procurement training or capacity development program.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this CY 2019 AAR with modification.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management enjoin the Municipal Accountant to strictly enforce the submission of required financial reports and accounts within the prescribed period.
observations.reason: This was due to lack of personnel in the Accounting Office.
The GSO allegedly conducted partial inventory only but no report was submitted to the Office of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management enjoin the Municipal Economic Enterprise Development Officer to prepare required financial reports in the LEE Manual to determine the financial condition and performance of the LGU’s Local Economic Enterprises.

We recommended that management require the BAC to prepare and submit the approved Procurement Monitoring Report to GPPB in printed and electronic format every semester as required in Section 12.2 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.reason: Still the Management does not prepare and submit its PMR to the GPPB for uploading to its website. Thus, reiterated in this CY 2019 AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Office along with the GAD Focal Point System to establish GAD Database for storage of necessary information in aid of effective gender-responsive planning, programming and policy information.
observations.managementAction: The LGU acquired necessary IT equipment to be used for the establishment of GAD Database.
observations.reason: Despite acquisition of the computer equipment, no database was still established.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management committed to secure from the Philippine Councilors’ League Incorporated, Vice Mayors’ League of the Philippines, and League of Municipalities of the Philippines to issue a government official receipt for all payments made to establish proper financial accountability.
observations.reason: The LGU is waiting for the National Officers of the Leagues of the local elective officials resolve the issue since the LGU is dependent on their action.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed to strictly comply with COA Circular No. 2009-001 to submit all copies of perfected contracts and purchase orders along with the required relevant supporting documents within five (5) working days from execution/approval; and

To strictly enforce the provisions of Section 16, Rule V of RA 9184 and its revised Implementing Rules and Regulations on the professionalization of BAC, TWG Members, and Procurement Units, whereby the HoPE shall ensure that they are sent to attend procurement training or capacity development program.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this CY 2019 AAR with modification.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendations that management:
Require immediate liquidation of all outstanding cash advances as of December 31, 2019 by issuing demand letters to the concerned parties;
Require the issuance of Certification by the Municipal Accountant that the previous cash advance has been liquidated and accounted for in the books before granting a new cash advance; and
Strictly adhere to the provisions on the granting and utilization of cash advances as prescribed by Section 89 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended that management:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer with the Local Chief Executive to make a representation to the Department of Finance requesting reconsideration and restructure on the penalties charged for possible reduction of the amount;
Enjoin the responsible officials in the monitoring of loans payable of the LGU to submit a written explanation thru the Local Chief Executive, why the penalty of P1,534,943.58 for late payments of DOF loan should not be a personal liability on their part; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to adjust the financial statements to recognize the unpaid penalties after setting-up appropriate cash back-up for the obligation.

We recommended that management:
Appropriate budget to satisfy obligations as agreed in the Compromise Agreement; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to adjust the financial statements to reflect the rights and obligations of the LGU on the Compromise Agreement entered into by the LGU and the plaintiff to settle the Civil Case No. 5316 on payment of just compensation of the land and damages.

We recommended that management:
Require the Municipal Accountant to effect adjusting entries to rectify the noted overstatement/understatement of the SET Receivables and their related accounts and observe the correct accounting entries in recording SET collections; and
Enjoin the Municipal Treasurer to regularly report to the Municipal Accountant the total amount of discounts and penalties on the RPT and SET as basis for recording to their appropriate accounts.

We recommended that management:
Direct the SIWASS Manager to submit report of collectibles monthly to the Municipal Accountant as basis for recording accrued income from the LGU’s services from its water facilities; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to apply Accrual Accounting in recognizing the Income from SIWASS and draw a Journal Entry Voucher to record unrecognized accrued revenue.

We reiterated our recommendations that management:
Require immediate liquidation of all outstanding cash advances as of December 31, 2019 by issuing demand letters to the concerned parties;
Require the issuance of Certification by the Municipal Accountant that the previous cash advance has been liquidated and accounted for in the books before granting a new cash advance; and
Strictly adhere to the provisions on the granting and utilization of cash advances as prescribed by Section 89 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended that management:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer with the Local Chief Executive to make a representation to the Department of Finance requesting reconsideration and restructure on the penalties charged for possible reduction of the amount;
Enjoin the responsible officials in the monitoring of loans payable of the LGU to submit a written explanation thru the Local Chief Executive, why the penalty of P1,534,943.58 for late payments of DOF loan should not be a personal liability on their part; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to adjust the financial statements to recognize the unpaid penalties after setting-up appropriate cash back-up for the obligation.

We recommended that management:
Appropriate budget to satisfy obligations as agreed in the Compromise Agreement; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to adjust the financial statements to reflect the rights and obligations of the LGU on the Compromise Agreement entered into by the LGU and the plaintiff to settle the Civil Case No. 5316 on payment of just compensation of the land and damages.

We recommended that management:
Require the Municipal Accountant to effect adjusting entries to rectify the noted overstatement/understatement of the SET Receivables and their related accounts and observe the correct accounting entries in recording SET collections; and
Enjoin the Municipal Treasurer to regularly report to the Municipal Accountant the total amount of discounts and penalties on the RPT and SET as basis for recording to their appropriate accounts.

We recommended that management:
Direct the SIWASS Manager to submit report of collectibles monthly to the Municipal Accountant as basis for recording accrued income from the LGU’s services from its water facilities; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to apply Accrual Accounting in recognizing the Income from SIWASS and draw a Journal Entry Voucher to record unrecognized accrued revenue.

We reiterated our recommendations that management:

Prohibit the granting of additional cash advance unless the previous cash advance given is first settled or proper accounting thereof is made;

Issue notice or demand letters to AOs to settle their accountabilities within the prescribed period.  In case of failure to comply, strictly impose sanctions such as withholding of salaries or any money due them; and

Request for write-off for those accounts that are deemed dormant and demand for liquidation is futile.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemented salary deductions on few employees with overdue cash advances.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this CY 2019 AAR with modification.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management enjoin the Municipal Accountant to maintain Subsidiary Records for each fund transfer received from National Government Agencies and other Local Government Units as trust funds.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant provided SL for all NGA and LGU funds received.
The collection for the delinquent accounts was intensified and now strictly enforced.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this CY 2019 AAR with modification.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

Request the Sangguniang Bayan to enact a Resolution prescribing the policies and guidelines on the grant of donation and financial assistance containing the following:
Kind of financial assistance (medical, funeral, etc.)
Approval limits for specific cases, including the frequency of grants to each individual
Criteria in granting the donation and the documentary requirements for each particular case
All cases should be referred to the MSWD for eligibility evaluation and the case study report prepared should be made part of the supporting documents of the claim

The Members of the Sangguniang Bayan should immediately stop the practice of granting financial assistance/donations to its constituents as this is not among its mandated duties and functions under Section 447 (a) of RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction:
Passed a Resolution for the policies and guidelines on the grant of donation and financial assistance.

The Sangguniang Bayan has already ceased its practice of giving donation and financial assistance using their SB Fund.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

Enjoin all job order workers to attach their actual accomplishment reports to their DTRs to be reviewed and verified by their respective immediate supervisors; and

Enjoin the Municipal Accountant to ensure that payments to job order workers are supported with their accomplishment reports for actual services rendered aside from duly approved DTRs.
observations.managementAction: The Management has now strictly enforced the “No DTR and Accomplishment Report, No Pay” policy.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management enjoin the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to develop/invest in high-impact capital expenditures in the utilization of the LGU’s 20% Development Fund as laid in the DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No, 2017-1.
observations.managementAction: The LGU has prepared an improved Development Plan.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this CY 2019 AAR with modification.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

Instruct the LDRRM Officer and Municipal Accountant to submit to COA on a monthly basis Report on the Utilization of LDRRMF using the prescribed format laid in COA Circular No. 2012-002; and

Enjoin the Municipal Accountant to post and update the Reports on the Sources and Utilizations of LDRRMF to the Municipality’s conspicuous places in furtherance of the Full Disclosure Policy as required under DILG Memorandum Circular (MC) No. 2012-141.
observations.managementAction: The Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund (DRRMF) were regularly furnished to the Office of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed to strictly enforce the Municipal Accountant to expedite completion of all bank reconciliation statements of the municipality to facilitate adjustments in the accounting and treasury books and to establish correct balances of cash accountability of the ICO – Municipal Treasurer relative to cash examination conducted; and

To require the ICO – Municipal Treasurer to return all unexpended balances of fund transfers to source government agencies and to facilitate closure of all dormant bank accounts in accordance with prescribed rules, laws, and regulations.  Further, to prepare inventory of obsolete checks for inspection and subsequent disposal.
observations.managementAction: Prepared all backlog Bank Reconciliation Statements and engaged a service of a JO personnel for the BRS preparation; returned the P300,000 to DA Regional Office for the Corn Mill project not implemented; and reminded the ICO-Treasurer to close dormant accounts.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant still incur delay in the preparation of BRS. Thus, reiterated in this CY 2019 AAR with modification.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management committed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to:

Coordinate with the Municipal Assessor on the updated data and information of Real Property Taxes for proper implementation of prescribed remedies and actions to generate more collections;

Initiate tax campaign in the different barangays of the municipality and enforce individual targets on all revenue collection personnel and periodically monitor their accomplishments; and

Regularly prepare and submit a certified list of all real property tax delinquencies to the sanggunian for the latter to assist in the enforcement of the remedies for collection.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Municipal Assessor, updated records on real property, conducted Tax Campaign in different Barangays, and conducted TOKTOK BUHIS together with the Provincial Treasurer’s Office Personnel. Notices of RPT delinquency were delivered. Lastly, certified list of RPT delinquents was prepared and submitted to the Sangguniang Bayan for assistance in the enforcement of the collection.
Returned the P300,000, representing the project cost of Corn Mill not implemented, to DA Region X.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to:

Strictly implement the recording of Public Infrastructure accounts from the registry to the municipal books in consonance to the provisions of Section 3.7 of COA Circular 2016-004; and 

Coordinate with the Office of the Municipal Engineer and obtain the updated list of completed projects of the municipality for prompt reconciliation of both records to support adjustments on the recognition of these Infrastructure Assets in the books.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant recorded the following Infrastructure Assets: 

1. Other Land Improvements - P2,336,612.60

2. Road Networks - P18,055,000.00

3. Flood control System - P800,000.00

4. Water Supply Systems P36,375,613.37

5. Seaport Systems P2,325,490

6.Other Infrastructure Projects - P1,076,540.00

7. Buildings - P7,270,000.00

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management committed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to:

Coordinate with the Municipal Assessor on the updated data and information of Real Property Taxes for proper implementation of prescribed remedies and actions to generate more collections;

Initiate tax campaign in the different barangays of the municipality and enforce individual targets on all revenue collection personnel and periodically monitor their accomplishments; and

Regularly prepare and submit a certified list of all real property tax delinquencies to the sanggunian for the latter to assist in the enforcement of the remedies for collection.

Notices of RPT delinquency were delivered. Lastly, certified list of RPT delinquents was prepared and submitted to the Sangguniang Bayan for assistance in the enforcement of the collection.
observations.reason: The Tax Campaign is still on going.

observations.managementAction: Returned the P300,000, representing the project cost of Corn Mill not implemented, to DA Region X.
Few accountable officers opted for salary deduction.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this CY 2018 AAR.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed to strictly comply with COA Circular No. 2009-001 to submit all copies of perfected contracts and purchase orders along with the required relevant supporting documents within five (5) working days from execution/approval; and

To strictly enforce the provisions of Section 16, Rule V of RA 9184 and its revised Implementing Rules and Regulations on the professionalization of BAC, TWG Members, and Procurement Units, whereby the HoPE shall ensure that they are sent to attend procurement training or capacity development program.
observations.managementAction: The BAC is exerting serious efforts for the on time submission of contracts.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:
We recommended and management agreed to strictly enforce the Municipal Accountant to expedite completion of all bank reconciliation statements of the municipality to facilitate adjustments in the accounting and treasury books and to establish correct balances of cash accountability of the ICO – Municipal Treasurer relative to cash examination conducted; and
To require the ICO – Municipal Treasurer to return all unexpended balances of fund transfers to source government agencies and to facilitate closure of all dormant bank accounts in accordance with prescribed rules, laws, and regulations.  Further, to prepare inventory of obsolete checks for inspection and subsequent disposal.

Recommendations:
We recommended and management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to:

Strictly implement the recording of Public Infrastructure accounts from the registry to the municipal books in consonance to the provisions of Section 3.7 of COA Circular 2016-004; and
Coordinate with the Office of the Municipal Engineer and obtain the updated list of completed projects of the municipality for prompt reconciliation of both records to support adjustments on the recognition of these Infrastructure Assets in the books.

Recommendations:
We recommended and management committed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to:

Coordinate with the Municipal Assessor on the updated data and information of Real Property Taxes for proper implementation of prescribed remedies and actions to generate more collections;
Initiate tax campaign in the different barangays of the municipality and enforce individual targets on all revenue collection personnel and periodically monitor their accomplishments; and
Regularly prepare and submit a certified list of all real property tax delinquencies to the sanggunian for the latter to assist in the enforcement of the remedies for collection.

Recommendation:
We recommended and management committed to secure from the Philippine Councilors’ League Incorporated, Vice Mayors’ League of the Philippines, and League of Municipalities of the Philippines to issue a government official receipt for all payments made to establish proper financial accountability.

Recommendations:
We recommended and management agreed to instruct the Human Resource Officer to strictly impose the aforecited CSC policy on government office hours for all regular and job order employees of the municipality and apply appropriate administrative sanctions if so warrant;
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that payrolls are duly supported with all the required documents before processing payment for salaries and wages for the period; and
Require all job order and casual employees hired during the period to execute Contract of Service indicating specific period of employment as mandated by law.

observations.managementAction: Demand letters were sent by registered mail and for employees still connected with the municipality, payroll deductions were regularly done.
observations.reason: Dormant accounts that are difficult to collect are still not submitted to facilitate request for write-off.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Office of the Municipal Engineer and obtain the updated list of completed projects of the municipality for prompt reconciliation of both records to support adjustments on the recognition of these Infrastructure Assets in the books.
observations.reason: This recommendation was not given much priority during the year therefore reiterated in Part II of this report with modifications.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed to strictly comply with COA Circular No. 2009-001 to submit all copies of perfected contracts and purchase orders along with the required relevant supporting documents within five (5) working days from execution/approval; and

To strictly enforce the provisions of Section 16, Rule V of RA 9184 and its revised Implementing Rules and Regulations on the professionalization of BAC, TWG Members, and Procurement Units, whereby the HoPE shall ensure that they are sent to attend procurement training or capacity development program.
observations.reason: Though contracts have been timely submitted to COA for the auditorial and legal review, however, documents attached were mostly incomplete, thus returned resulting to delay in the conduct of review.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Office of the Municipal Engineer and obtain the updated list of completed projects of the municipality for prompt reconciliation of both records to support adjustments on the recognition of these Infrastructure Assets in the books.

We recommended and management agreed to strictly comply with COA Circular No. 2009-001 to submit all copies of perfected contracts and purchase orders along with the required relevant supporting documents within five (5) working days from execution/approval; and
To strictly enforce the provisions of Section 16, Rule V of RA 9184 and its revised Implementing Rules and Regulations on the professionalization of BAC, TWG Members, and Procurement Units, whereby the HoPE shall ensure that they are sent to attend procurement training or capacity development program.

The property officer should maintain a record of property card for each item of PPE.

The municipality should conduct physical count of all PPEs owned by the agency, through its duly designated members of the Inventory Committee, at least once a year.
Reconciliation should be done by the Accounting Unit and the Property Officer from time to time.
observations.managementAction:
AAR CY 2015 | The LGU designated a General Services Officer at the Office of Treasury, and the office was not yet created.

Conducted physical count as of December 31, 2016.  Submission of Inventory Items Utilization is still on-going.
Completed projects were added to PPE.  But, Infrastructure Assets booked in a separate registries were not yet taken up in the books as required in PPSAS.
observations.reason: Validity, existence, ownership and valuation of the LGU’s PPE still cannot be ascertained thus reiterated in Part II of the report.

observations.recommendation:
Conduct monthly monitoring of liquidation of cash advances.

Refrain from allowing municipal personnel for additional cash advance unless their previous cash advance is settled.

Impose administrative disciplinary action, to municipal officials and employee for the settlement of their outstanding accounts/ liquidation of their respective cash advances after demands were made.
observations.managementAction:
AAR CY 2015 | Cash advances were monitored by the accounting office on a monthly and quarterly basis.

Demand letters shall also be sent to officers and employees with long outstanding balances.
observations.reason: The sanction to withhold salaries of personnel as liquidation on their long outstanding balances was not implemented, thus reiterated in Part II of the report.

observations.recommendation:
Direct the accounting office to maintain subsidiary ledgers provided for each account to support as per controlling account in the general ledger for easy verification and control of the account, to facilitate review of transactions and events recorded and to obtain reliability of financial records and reports.

Improve the present accounting system by installing computerized system of the accounting office that will provide adequate data control and present accurate reports.
observations.managementAction:
AAR CY 2015 | The accounting office had hired one additional employee to facilitate and assist timely recording of transactions.


Currently optimizing simple computer applications available in the accounting office.

observations.recommendation:
Require the accountable officer to prepare monthly, the Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms and submit the same to the municipal treasurer.

Require the municipal treasurer to prepare the consolidated Inventory and Statement of Accountability for Accountable Forms and submit it to the Unit Auditor not later that the fifth day of the ensuing month as required by COA Circular No. 2015-009.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2015 | The Office of the Municipal Treasurer maintains logbook for all accountable forms purchased and issued to AOs.  Then all AOs are required to submit RAAF to the Municipal Treasurer periodically and submit the consolidated RAAF to the Unit Auditor.

observations.recommendation: We recommend to management that an Internal Audit Service be created and organized independent of the Accounting Department, to perform staff functions in accordance with the above provisions of R.A. No. 4177 and Administrative Order Nos. 70 and 278. Moreover, it should be required to regularly evaluate and/or monitor the implementation of its internal accounting and operational controls in all aspects of its operations to ensure that reliability of data, assets and records are safeguarded, and that operational efficiency and adherence to prescribed policies are promoted.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2015 | The LGU is planning to create Internal Audit Services Unit.  The staff from Office of the Mayor will be designated to perform internal audit independent from the accounting office.

observations.recommendation:
Require the concerned LGUs and NGAs to submit the documentary requirements, where applicable, as prescribed under Section 3.0 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

Subsequently, correcting entries should be effected on the recording of funds released to the various LGUs and NGAs. Hence, the accounts Due from LGUs and Due from NGAs should be debited to record the release of funds to LGUs and NGAs for the implementation of projects/ programs/ activities, and credit these accounts for the liquidation of the subject funds.

Refrain from incurring unnecessary expenditure of government funds to preclude loss of wastage thereof and limit expenses to those which are essential and supportive of the implementation of the objectives and mission of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: |  | The financial assistance granted by the LGU to indigents, other LGUs, and private entities were treated as donations.  Those granted to other LGUs and barangays, liquidations were required and were accounted and recorded accordingly.
